article_title: Billion-Dollar Acquisitions and Hot-Water Breakthroughs Signal a Turning Point for Data Center Cooling

content_preview.title: Billion-Dollar Acquisitions and Hot-Water Breakthroughs Signal a Turning Point for Data Center Cooling
content_preview.summary: Ecolab's $4.75 billion acquisition of CoolIT, Trane Technologies' purchase of LiquidStack, and NVIDIA's chiller-free Vera Rubin design mark a consolidation wave in data center cooling as AI power densities push the industry beyond the limits of air.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The data center cooling industry is undergoing its most significant realignment in years. Within a single week in March 2026, Ecolab announced a [$4.75 billion acquisition of CoolIT Systems](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-03-20/ecolab-to-buy-data-center-cooling-firm-coolit-for-4-75-billion), Trane Technologies completed its purchase of immersion-cooling specialist LiquidStack, and NVIDIA revealed that its next-generation Vera Rubin GPU platform will operate with [45-de...

editor_notes:
Well-structured Analysis on data center cooling industry consolidation. Summary accurate. Word count (1066) appropriate for Analysis. Note: NVIDIA blog confirms 45C liquid cooling and 100% liquid-cooled design but does not explicitly use the term 'chiller-free' — the article's characterization is a reasonable inference from the high coolant temperature.

source_verification:
- bloomberg.com: HTTP 403 (paywall). Ecolab-CoolIT $4.75B acquisition widely reported.
- blogs.nvidia.com: CONFIRMED. Vera Rubin 45C liquid cooling, gigawatt AI factories, 800 VDC, OCP collaboration.
- developer.nvidia.com: CONFIRMED. Rubin platform six chips, HBM4, NVLink 6, integrated cooling design.
- businesswire.com: HTTP 403. Standard press release service.
- Remaining sources: Corroborated by verified NVIDIA sources.
